name: Holo Tube
summary: Stream video natively as verified objects. Every segment is checked genuine before it reaches the decoder — no iframe, no trust in the source, just bytes that prove themselves.
comment_shared: Holo Tube composes the OS κ-feed (holo-media.mjs over holo-kstore.js) — every media segment is resolved BY ITS κ and re-derived before decode. The demo MediaGraph + its fMP4 segments are vendored under media/ so they seal into the app closure and resolve at /.holo/sha256/<hex> offline.

description.p: A video player with no foreign runtime and no trust in the origin. A stream is immutable per segment — an init segment plus an ordered list of media segments — so a video is a finite content-addressed DAG (a MediaGraph) and a playlist or radio queue is an open DAG walked through 'next'.

Every segment is pulled BY ITS κ through the OS κ-store (arena → OPFS → /.holo route → IPFS heal) and re-derived against that κ before a single byte reaches the decoder (Law L5). A tampered segment is refused — playback stalls rather than plays bad bytes. Segments are bit-exact to source; nothing is transcoded on the identity path.

ul: Native MSE playback — no iframe embed
κ-verified segments: every played byte re-derives to its address
Offline replay: a watched video plays from the OPFS κ-store, origin dead
Gapless playlist / radio: the next video's κ is buffered before this one ends
Source-agnostic ingest: any DASH/HLS origin; YouTube is one adapter
A live 'verified ✓ N segments' counter — the L5 flex made visible

releases.description: Native κ-addressed video streaming in Holo Hub. A MediaGraph seals a stream as a content-addressed DAG of fMP4/WebM segments; the MSE κ-feed (holo-media.mjs) resolves each segment by its κ and re-derives it before decode (Law L5). VOD seals finite, radio walks 'next' gapless. Bit-exact segments, offline replay from OPFS, source-agnostic ingest (DASH/HLS; YouTube adapter).
